info.title: TravelDesk Agent API
info.description:
![AGENT API](https://tdms.websitetravel.com/images/api-hero-banner.jpg)

# Agent API
**Sell travel through your website / platform via our simple Agent API**

---

## Overview

TravelDesk represents over 3,300 Tourism Operators; listing more than 6,500 activities, tours, accommodation and transport options across Australasia. TravelDesk is technology designed to facilitate transactions between agents and operators.

The Agent API is for businesses wanting to on-sell tours, activities and accommodation through their own website or internal reservation platform.

Via the API we seamlessly connect your:

**Your Systems:**
- Website
- App  
- Control Management System (CMS)
- Property Management System (PMS)
- Reservation system
- Booking platform

**With Our Services:**
- Excellent wholesale rates
- Up to date content
- Product images
- Live availability
- Booking and confirmation
- Thousands of experiences

## Integration Process

### 🔐 GET ACCESS
View our published API documentation.
Request the login credentials and get access to the API and our test server.

### 🛠️ DEVELOP & TEST
Consume our Agent API directly into your platform/site/app.
Approximate development time for a simple integration has been within 10 days followed by testing.

### 🚀 TEST & RELEASE
Shared media release and subsequent campaign to promote the new connection to relevant stakeholders.

## Environments
- **Test**: https://tdmstest.websitetravel.com
- **Production**: https://tdms.websitetravel.com

## Data Format
The API accepts requests and returns responses in **JSON format only**! All incoming requests must have an `Accept: application/json` header. An error message with a 406 HTTP status code will be returned if this is omitted.

## Authentication
The WebsiteTravel API uses **OAuth2 authentication**. To acquire a token, call the `POST /apiv1/token` route, including the client_id and client_secret provided to you by WebsiteTravel upon signup.
This token should then be included in every subsequent request.

## Products
### Basics
The Product database can be searched using the `GET /apiv1/products` method. Each Product will have one or more fares associated with it. The primary key of a fare is the `productPricesDetailsId` field. Most methods in our API take the ID of a fare as parameters, rather than a Product.

### Caching Products
Some developers prefer to cache a list of Website Travel products in order to reduce the number of queries that they make to the Website Travel API. To support this we provide a `GET /apiv1/product/lastupdate` method, which can be used to check whether any changes have been made to the product since you retrieved it from the API.

## Packages
Products with productClass 'Z' are packages. Each of the components of the package is known as an 'option'. An option contains one or more Products; when it contains more than one Product, the customer can choose between these Products. You can use the `GET /apiv1/package` method to find out what options are in a package.

## Live Availability
Some of our Fares support live availability querying. These Products can be identified because the `bookingSystem` field will not be null. You can find out what time slots the Product has by calling `GET /bookingdetails`.


## Placing an Order
The first step of placing an order is to generate a booking reference by calling POST /bookingreference. You should then include this when you call POST /order. If order submission fails for any reason, you will need to generate a new booking reference before you resubmit the order.

The POST /order method takes a paymentMethod parameter. The available payment methods vary between agents. You can see what payment methods you have access to by looking at /paymentmethods.
        

### Order Placement Workflows

The API supports two distinct order placement workflows depending on your payment processing needs:

#### 🏪 Offline Payment Workflow (Simple)
For cash, bank transfer, or external payment processing:

1. `POST /apiv1/bookingreference` - Generate booking reference
2. `GET /apiv1/referralsources` - Get referral options *(optional - depends on reseller)*
3. `GET /apiv1/agent/paymentmethods` - Get payment methods
4. `POST /apiv1/validateCart` - Validate cart before submission
5. `POST /apiv1/order` - Submit order directly (`processAsQuote: false`)

#### 💳 Online Payment Workflow (Full)
For credit card processing via payment gateway:

1. `POST /apiv1/bookingreference` - Generate booking reference  
2. `GET /apiv1/referralsources` - Get referral options *(optional - depends on reseller)*
3. `GET /apiv1/agent/paymentmethods` - Get online payment methods
4. `POST /apiv1/validateCart` - Validate cart before submission
5. `POST /apiv1/order` - Create quote (`processAsQuote: true`)
6. `GET /apiv1/validateQuote` - Validate quote integrity
7. `GET /apiv1/pgw/authenticate` - Get payment gateway URL
8. `POST /apiv1/order/{bookingReference}/convertQuote` - Convert to order after payment

### Important Notes
- Always validate cart contents before order submission
- Use unique booking references for each order attempt
- Online payments require quote-to-order conversion after successful payment
- If order submission fails, generate a new booking reference before retry
- **Referral sources are optional** - depends on reseller configuration; if no referral sources are available, skip step 2 and omit referralSourceId from order submission

### 📊 Interactive Workflow Documentation
**[View Visual Flowcharts →](/api/workflows/order-placement)**

## Payment Gateway
Website Travel provides an online credit card payment facility known as the Payment Gateway. Some configuration is required before you can use the Payment Gateway; please contact Website Travel to arrange for this to happen.

To use the Payment Gateway, you should call POST /pgw/authenticate to obtain a payment URL, and then redirect the user to this page so that they can fill in their card details. Please note that you are only permitted to make one payment per order through the Payment Gateway.

This API method takes a returnUrl parameter, and you will receive a GET request on this URI once the payment has been processed. The request will include a cmsgw_TxnResponseCode parameter - a value of 0 indicates that the payment was successful, while any other value indicates that an error occurred.


## Support
If you have any queries about this API, please contact **api@websitetravel.com**.

---

**Start exploring the API endpoints below** ⬇️

get.description:
Get a list of payment methods that the agent supports. Includes online payment configuration fields where this is enabled. This is typically the third step in the order placement workflow.

**Request URL**: `GET /apiv1/agent/paymentmethods`

**Authentication Required**: Yes - Bearer token in Authorization header

**Usage in Order Flow**:
1. Get booking reference (`POST /apiv1/bookingreference`)
2. Get referral sources (`GET /apiv1/referralsources`) - optional
3. **Get payment methods** (`GET /apiv1/agent/paymentmethods`) - this endpoint
4. Validate cart (`POST /apiv1/validateCart`)
5. Submit order (`POST /apiv1/order`)

#### Response

**Top Level Result Object**

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| paymentMethods | n | Array (Objects) | Array of paymentMethod objects. |

**paymentMethod Object**

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| journalAccountId | n | Integer | ID of journal account/payment method. |
| name | n | String | Human-readable name of payment method. |
| code | n | String | API reference code of payment method (can be passed into POST /order). |
| supportsOnlinePayment | n | boolean | Whether this payment method supports online payments taken via the Website Travel payment gateway. |
| paymentReturnUrl | y | String | Hostname currently registered for payment attempt returnUrls. |

200.description: List of available payment methods for the agent

supportsOnlinePayment.description: Whether this method supports online payments via payment gateway

paymentReturnUrl.description: Hostname registered for payment return URLs

get.summary: Get booking details for a fareprice
get.description:
Get lists of booking times, pickup locations and optional fields to be used when making a booking for the fareprice. Also provides some basic contact details for the supplier. Examples: GET /apiv1/bookingdetails/20322250, GET /apiv1/bookingdetails/17602092

#### Response

**Top Level Result Object**

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| bookingTimes | y | Array (Objects) | Array of bookingTime objects. |
| pickupLocations | y | Array (Objects) | Array of pickupLocation objects. |
| optionalFields | y | Array (Objects) | Array of optionalField objects |
| supplier | y | Object | Supplier object. |
| imagePath | n | String | Full URL of product thumbnail. |

**bookingTime Object**

A booking time indicates a time slot for which a booking may be made (eg "11am", "2pm"). You will need to pass the desired booking time into the `GET /checkavailabilityrange` method to find out whether the product can be booked for your preferred dates, and into `POST /order` when you place the order.

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| BookingTimeID | n | String | Booking Time ID used by booking system. |
| BookingTime | n | String | Time of Booking as returned from the booking system. May or may not be an actual time (for example, an accommodation product doesn't have the concept of separate times so it might return a string like "Daily availability"). |
| Default | y | Boolean | Indicates that this Booking Time is the default for the fareprice. This may be used by products that only have one booking time, and indicates that it is not necessary to display the time to the user. For example, when booking an accommodation product you can assume that the customer wants to use the default 'time', without having to ask them. |

**pickupLocation Object**

A pickup location gives a time and location where a customer may request to be picked up from. If the product's supplier does not provide pickups, the array of pickup locations will be empty. Where this service is provided, you will need to pass information about your chosen pickup location into `POST /order`.

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| PickupID | n | String | ID of Pickup used by booking. |
| PickupTime | y | String | Time of Pickup. |
| PickupLocation | n | String | Pickup location text. |

**optionalField Object**

An optional field may be used by the supplier to request some additional information about the booking, for example dietary requirements. If the product does not have any optional fields, this array will be empty. Where optional fields are specified, you should collect this information from the customer and pass it into `POST /order`.

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| optionalFieldId | n | Integer | Unique ID of the Optional field |
| title | n | String | Title of optional field |

**supplier Object**

The supplier object contains contact details for the product's supplier.

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| name | n | String | Name of Supplier. |
| phone | y | String | Phone number of Supplier. |
| freePhone | y | String | Alternate phone number of Supplier. |

#### Requirements

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| productPricesDetailsId | integer | The Product Price Details ID of the fareprice. |

#### Status Codes

| Status Code | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| [200](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HTTP_200) | • Returned when successful. |
| [400](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HTTP_400) | • Returned when the fareprice does not support live availability. |
| [403](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HTTP_403) | • Returned when the user is not authorized. |
| [404](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HTTP_404) | • Returned when the fareprice does not exist. |
| [500](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HTTP_500) | • Returned when invalid combination of parameters is issued. |

get.summary: Get a list of categories of a given type
get.description:
## Get a list of categories of a given type ##

Get a list of categories that belong to the given category type. The IDs of these categories can then be passed into GET /products to get a list of products which belong to those categories. For example, to get a list of all dorm-style accommodation in Brisbane, you would find the region category ID for "Brisbane" and the accommodation category for "dorms" and use these as filters for GET /products.

There are four category types that tell you where a product is - country, state, region and locality. These categories are hierarchical - each state belongs to a country. However, it is possible to "skip" layers in the hierarchy, so a region or a locality may belong directly to a country. If you want to see a list of all localities for a country, you should use the 'countries' parameter rather than finding all the regions for that country and passing them in as the 'regions' parameter.

There are also four other category types that give you more information type of product. Each product belongs to a productclass, which divides the products up into groups such as transport, day activity, merchandise or accommodation. Products may also have multiple accommodation, transport and activity types. In the case of these last three categories, you can use this method to find out what types of accommodation/transport/activities are available in a geographic area - for example, you can call GET /categories/activities?locality=123 to find out what types of activities can be purchased in locality 123.

#### Response #### **Top Level Result Object**
Field name Nullable Type Description
resultsnArray (Objects)Array of Category objects.
**Category**
Field name Nullable Type Description
idnIntegerID of Category.
textnStringName of Category.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Type of category to retrieve. Available types and example requests:
                
**countries** - `GET /apiv1/categories/countries?term=Aust`  
**states** - `GET /apiv1/categories/states?countries=20`  
**regions** - `GET /apiv1/categories/regions?states=24`  
**localities** - `GET /apiv1/categories/localities?regions=65`  
**activities** - `GET /apiv1/categories/activities`  
**accommodation** - `GET /apiv1/categories/accommodation`  
**transport** - `GET /apiv1/categories/transport`  
**productclasses** - `GET /apiv1/categories/productclasses`

get.description: Search for travel products using various filters including name, location, categories, and date ranges. Results can be sorted and paginated.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Returns all Products which match a given set of keywords. Multiple keywords can be separated with spaces (e.g. 'sailing Whitsundays') and only Products which match all of the keywords will be returned.

parameters.name: supplier
parameters.description: Returns all Products which belong to a given set of Suppliers. Multiple Suppliers can be included in one query using a comma-separated list of IDs. Refer GET /suppliers

parameters.name: packages
parameters.description: Limits the search to packages. You may pass in keywords as per the 'name' parameter, which will limit the search to packages which match that keyword. Alternatively, you can use the 'packages' parameter without a value ('packages=') and use the other filter parameters to limit your search.

parameters.name: countries
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Country IDs. Refer GET /categories/countries

parameters.name: states
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of State IDs. Refer GET /categories/states

parameters.name: regions
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Region IDs. Refer GET /categories/regions

parameters.name: localities
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Locality IDs. A locality is a town, city or suburb within a region. Refer GET /categories/locality

parameters.name: accommodation
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Accommodation Type IDs. Refer GET /categories/accommodation

parameters.name: activities
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Activity Type IDs.

parameters.name: transport
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Transport Type IDs.

parameters.name: productclass
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Product Class IDs.

parameters.name: minLat
parameters.description: Limit results to products north of this latitude.

parameters.name: maxLat
parameters.description: Limit results to products south of this latitude.

parameters.name: minLng
parameters.description: Limit results to products east of this longitude.

parameters.name: maxLng
parameters.description: Limit results to products west of this longitude.

parameters.name: travelStart
parameters.description: Limit results to products with fares available on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD format).

parameters.name: travelEnd
parameters.description: Limit results to products with fares available on or before this date (YYYY-MM-DD format).

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Length of trip in days

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Field to sort results by

parameters.name: sortDirection
parameters.description: Sort direction

parameters.name: records-start
parameters.description: Position of first result to return (for pagination).

parameters.name: records-length
parameters.description: Number of results to return (max 100).

parameters.name: shortSearch
parameters.description: Return shortened results without fare information for faster response.

get.description:
Get details on a specific product by productId. Returns complete product information including fares, categories, itinerary, and booking details.

**Request URL**: `GET /apiv1/product/{productIds}`

**Example**: `GET /apiv1/product/26401`

**Multiple Products**: `GET /apiv1/product/26401,18673,12345`

**Authentication Required**: Yes - Bearer token in Authorization header

#### Response

**Top Level Result Object**

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| fetchedRecords | n | Integer | Number of records fetched for this request. |
| totalDisplayRecords | n | Integer | Total number of matching records found. Note that this is an indicator only, and occasionally the actual number of matching results may be lower than this. |
| totalRecords | n | Integer | Total number of Products in the database. |
| results | n | Array (Objects) | Array of Product objects. |

**Product Object**

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| extras | y | String | Optional extra (HTML). |
| instructions | y | String | Instructions to the customer (HTML). |
| favourite | n | Boolean | Is this a favourite Product. |
| pickups | y | String | Pickup details text. |
| productImagePath | y | String | URL to main Product image. |
| itinerary | y | String | Itinerary information (HTML). |
| productClass | y | String | One character code indicating Product Class (see table below). |
| productDetails | n | String | Details of Product to show customer (HTML). |
| productId | n | Integer | Product ID. |
| conditions | y | String | Any conditions on Product (HTML). |
| name | n | String | Display name of the Product. |
| productTeaser | n | String | Short description of Product (HTML). |
| ticketEntryFieldDescription | y | String | Description for ID input for external system Products. |
| ticketEntryFieldFormat | y | String | Used to limit ID input entry for external system Products (JSON). |
| ticketEntryFieldName | y | String | Label text for external system Products (JSON). |
| noRefunds | n | Integer | Whether Product can be refunded. |
| noRefundMessage | y | String | Message to display when a Product is not refundable. |
| categories | n | Array (Objects) | Array of Category objects. |
| address | y | Object | Optional Address object related to Product. |
| faresprices | n | Array (Objects) | Array of FarePrice objects. |
| supplierName | n | String | Name of Supplier Product belongs to. |
| apiProviderId | n | Integer | Indicates product fetches information externally. |
| groupFaresForAvailabilityCheck | n | Integer | Indicates that products fares can be searched grouped, using a fare type and a range of dates. See 'Live Availability Bulk Checking' at top of page. |
| supplierid | n | Integer | Supplier identification. |
| countryId | n | Integer | Country identification. |
| minAgeRecommendation | n | Integer | Minimum recommended age. |
| maxAgeRecommendation | n | Integer | Maximum recommended age. |
| ageRestriction | n | String | Description of any age restrictions (e.g. for an R18 hostel). |
| fitnessRequirement | n | String | Description of fitness requirements. |
| minGroupSize | n | Integer | Minimum tour group size. |
| maxGroupSize | n | Integer | Maximum tour group size. |
| dietaryRequirements | n | Integer | Dietary requirements. |
| luggageRequirements | n | String | Luggage requirements. |
| includedItems | n | Array(String) | Description of items included in fare. |
| bringItems | n | Array(String) | Description of items the guest is expected to bring. |
| voucherTerms | y | String | Description of terms and conditions, disclaimers etc. |
| weekDays | y | Array(String) | Comma-separated list of days of the week that trip departs. This field is "Deprecated", recommended to use "departureDates" field. |
| departureDates | y | Array | Tour departure dates as array. (e.g ["2018-06-09","2018-06-16","2018-06-23"]). Date format of individual departure date is "YYYY-MM-DD". |
| recurrenceDate | y | String | Frequency of departure recurrence (e.g. "Weekly", "Daily", "Monthly"). This field is "Deprecated", recommended to use "departureDates" field. |
| specialCircumstanceDate | y | String | Description of special dates which affect departure info (e.g. Christmas Day). This field is "Deprecated", recommended to use "departureDates" field. |
| operatingPartner | y | String | Operating partner. |
| tourCode | y | String | Tour code. |
| accommodationStyle | y | String | Description of accommodation style. |
| vehicleType | y | String | Description of vehicle type. |
| mandatoryFieldCheckout | y | Array(string) | Indicates these customer field information are mandatory for this product. |
| isCachecowSupported | n | Boolean | Indicates whether this product supports CacheCow. |
| cachecowCalendarSupport | n | Boolean | Indicates whether this product supports CacheCow calendar. |
| productRanking | n | Integer | Product ranking for display order. |

**Product Classes**

| Product Class Name | Product Class Key | Product Description |
|--------------------|-------------------|---------------------|
| Activity | V | Activity only Product. |
| Merchandise | M | Merchandise. |
| Accommodation | A | Accommodation Product. |
| Tour Product | Y | Different Tour Products. |
| Transport | T | Transportation. |

**Category Object**

Category names can be retrieved using the appropriate call to GET /categories/{name}.

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| countries | n | Array | Array of Country IDs. |
| countryCodes | n | Array | Array of ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes. |
| states | n | Array | Array of State IDs. |
| regions | n | Array | Array of Region IDs. |
| locations | n | Array | Array of Location IDs. |
| accommodation | n | Array | Array of Accommodation type IDs. |
| activities | n | Array | Array of Activity IDs. |
| transport | n | Array | Array of Transport IDs. |
| startLocation | n | Integer | ID of start location. |
| endLocation | n | Integer | ID of end location. |

**FarePrice**

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| fareName | n | String | Name of Fare. |
| fareTypeCategory | y | String | Name of the Fare Type Category. |
| numAdults | y | Integer | Number of adults eligible to travel on fare (applies to Family category fares). |
| numChildren | y | Integer | Number of children eligible to travel on fare (applies to Family category fares). |
| childMinAge | y | Integer | Minimum age for children eligible to travel on fare (applies to Child and Family category fares). |

get.description:
Get a list of product updates with filtering and sorting options. Example: GET /apiv1/products/lastUpdate?sortBy=lastUpdated&sortDirection=desc&updatedAfter=2026-02-13

#### Parameter Note

Searches can be further refined by supplying arguments for product IDs and/or after date/time.

**Top Level Result Object**

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| fetchedRecords | n | Integer | Number of product updates fetched for this request. |
| totalRecords | n | String | Total number of matching products with updates in the Website Travel database. |
| results | n | Array (Objects) | Array of Product Update objects. |

**Product Update**

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |
|------------|----------|------|-------------|
| productId | n | Integer | Product ID |
| lastUpdated | n | String | An ISO8601 DateTime string containing the last update of product. |

***Sorting Results***

The returned results can be sorted by the id and lastUpdate fields. In order to sort the results append two extra arguments to the body "sortBy" and "sortDirection". An example of a search sorted by the lastUpdated in descending order would be `{"sortBy": "lastUpdated", "sortDirection": "desc"}`

parameters.description: Start of results to return

parameters.name: recordLength
parameters.description: Length of results to return

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Name of field to sort by (allowed values are 'productId' and 'lastUpdated')

parameters.name: sortDirection
parameters.description: Direction to sort in (allowed values are 'asc' and 'desc')

parameters.name: updatedBefore
parameters.description: An ISO8601 DateTime string (e.g. '2013-12-12T23:00:46+0000') to limit results to before a certain datetime.

parameters.name: updatedAfter
parameters.description: An ISO8601 DateTime string (e.g. '2013-12-12T23:00:46+0000') to limit results to after a certain datetime.

parameters.name: productIds
parameters.description: Array of integers containing product IDs to limit the results to.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Country IDs.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of State IDs.

parameters.name: regions
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Region IDs.

parameters.name: localities
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Locality IDs. A locality is a town, city or suburb within a region.

get.summary: Check availability of a given fareprice
get.description:
Find out how much availability the given fareprice has for a specified date range. This request allows you to check availability for more than one date at the same time. Examples: GET /apiv1/checkavailabilityrange/20322250/1/18-Mar-2026/4, GET /apiv1/checkavailabilityrange/17602092/07_00_00/18-Mar-2026/4, GET /apiv1/checkavailabilityrange/9487875/08:00:00/18-Mar-2026/4 #### Response **Top Level Result Object** | Field name | Nullable | Type | Description | |------------|----------|------|-------------| | n/a | y | Array (Objects) | Array of daily availability objects - keyed by Product Prices Details Id. | **Daily Availability Object** | Field name | Nullable | Type | Description | |------------|----------|------|-------------| | datePriceCacheId | y | Integer | Identifier of special price cache for booking date from availability lookup. Used when booking multiple dates with different prices. | | productPricesDetailsId | n | Integer | ID of ProductPricesDetails (primary key to Product Detail). | | BookingDate | n | String | Date availability for in format "01-Dec-2013". | | EndDate | y | String | End date for multi-day products in format "01-Dec-2013". | | BookingTimeID | y | String | ID of the Booking time. | | NumAvailable | n | Integer | Places available for that date. There are three types of return values:
• **-1**: The tour does not run on this day.
• **0**: There is no availability remaining for this booking time on the given date.
• **>=1**: There are X number of places available for this booking time on the given date. | | NumAvailableDisplay | n | String | Display-friendly version of available places (e.g., "10+" for 22 available). | | Note | y | String | Additional information about availability or booking requirements. | | RequiresFurtherDetailsToConfirm | n | Boolean | Indicates if additional details (pickup/dropoff/time selection) are required before confirming availability. If true, call /detailsToConfirmAvailability. | | DateRestriction | y | Object | Date Restriction object containing booking rules for the date. | | productPricingData | n | Object | Pricing information object containing RRP, NetRate, and currency details. | | fields | n | Array | Array of field objects for booking systems requiring further selection (pickup, dropoff, time slots). | | extraInfo | n | Array | Additional booking information such as levy fees or special requirements. | **productPricingData Object** | Field name | Nullable | Type | Description | |------------|----------|------|-------------| | RRP | n | Float | Recommended retail price in agent's currency. | | NetRate | n | Float | Agent net rate in agent's currency. | | FareCurrencyRRP | y | Float | Recommended retail price in fare's original currency. | | FareCurrencyNetRate | y | Float | Net rate in fare's original currency. | | IgnorePpdMargins | n | Boolean | Indicates if product pricing detail margins should be ignored. | | addOnPricing | n | Array | Array of additional pricing components or add-ons. | **fields Object (when RequiresFurtherDetailsToConfirm = true)** | Field name | Nullable | Type | Description | |------------|----------|------|-------------| | date | n | String | Date this field applies to in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | type | n | String | Type of field: "radio" for single selection, "select" for dropdown options. | | name | n | String | Field name (timeId, pickupId, dropoffId) used when making bookings in POST /order. | | displayName | n | String | Human-readable field label for UI display. | | required | n | Boolean | Whether this field is mandatory for booking. | | options | n | Array | Array of option objects containing available selections. | | timeId | y | Integer | Associated booking time ID (present for pickup/dropoff fields). | **options Object (within fields)** | Field name | Nullable | Type | Description | |------------|----------|------|-------------| | externalId | n | String/Integer | External identifier to use in booking requests. | | name | y | String | Internal name of the option. | | displayName | n | String | Human-readable option label for UI display. | | numAvailable | y | Integer | Number available for this option (for time slots). | | note | y | String | Additional notes or requirements for this option. | | transfer | y | String | Transfer information for pickup locations (CustomLinc). | **extraInfo Object** | Field name | Nullable | Type | Description | |------------|----------|------|-------------| | levy | y | String | Additional levy fee information (e.g., "AUD $15"). | ***Date Restriction Object*** | Field name | Nullable | Type | Description | |------------|----------|------|-------------| | minimumConsecutiveDays | n | Integer | Default one. Minimum number of consecutive days (inclusive) that must be booked. | | date | y | String | Date restriction applies to. | | canArrive | n | Integer | "0" indicates not able to start on this date. | | canDepart | n | Integer | "0" indicates not able to finish on this date. |

| name | n | String | Internal name. |
| displayName | n | String | Display friendly name. |
| options | n | Array | Array of options with supported values. |
| required | n | Boolean | Whether field is required to make a booking. |

***Option Object***

| Field name | Nullable | Type | Description |

| externalId | n | String | External identifier. |
| name | n | String | Internal name. |
| displayName | n | String | Display friendly name. |
| numAvailable | n | Integer | Number of available slots for this option. Note this is only relevant for required fields. |
| note | y | String | Extra details if provided. |

get.description:
Check real-time availability for products that support live availability checking. This endpoint allows you to verify availability for specific dates and booking times before making a booking.

Response

Top Level Result Object

The response is a date-indexed object where each key is a date (YYYY-MM-DD format) containing an array of Availability objects for that date.

Field name Nullable Type Description
date (YYYY-MM-DD)nArray (Objects)Array of Availability Result objects for the specified date. Each date in the requested range will have its own key.

Availability Result Object

Field name Nullable Type Description
ProductPricesDetailsIdnIntegerThe Product Price Details ID that was queried.
BookingTimeIdnStringIdentifier for the booking time slot. Use this when making a reservation or checking pricing.
DateIndexnStringThe date for this availability result in YYYY-MM-DD format.
NumAvailablenIntegerNumber of spaces available. -1 indicates tour does not run on this day, 0 means sold out, positive numbers indicate available spaces.
NumAvailableDisplaynStringDisplay-friendly version of NumAvailable. May show '0' for -1 values.
FieldsnArray (Objects)Array of Field objects containing options that need to be selected (e.g., time, pickup, dropoff, addons).
RequiresPricingChecknBooleanWhether the fare requires a check to GET /checkAvailability/{productPricesDetailsId}/pricingData once options (e.g. pickup, dropoff) have been selected.
RequiresReservationChecknBooleanWhether the fare requires a check to GET /checkAvailability/{productPricesDetailsId}/reservation once options (e.g. pickup, dropoff) have been selected.
ProductIdnIntegerThe Product ID.
FareNamenStringName of the fare/price option.
ProductNamenStringName of the product.
SuccessnBooleanIndicates if the availability check was successful.
CacheAvailabilitynBooleanIndicates if this availability data is cached or real-time.

Field Object

Fields represent options that may need to be selected by the user (e.g., time slots, pickup locations, addons).

Field name Nullable Type Description
displayNamenStringDisplay-friendly name for the field (e.g., 'Time', 'Pickup', 'Dropoff', 'Secondary/Extra Fare').
namenStringInternal name for the field (e.g., 'timeId', 'pickupId', 'dropoffId', 'addonId').
optionsnArray (Objects)Array of Option objects representing available choices for this field.
requirednBooleanWhether this field must be selected before booking.
timeIdyStringAssociated time ID if this field is time-dependent (e.g., pickup options may vary by time slot).

Option Object

Field name Nullable Type Description
defaultnBooleanWhether this option is selected by default.
displayNamenStringDisplay-friendly name for the option (shown to user).
externalIdnStringExternal identifier for the option. Use this value when making bookings or checking pricing/reservations.
nameyStringInternal name for the option.

Important Notes:

  • NumAvailable values: -1 = tour doesn't run this day, 0 = sold out, positive number = available spaces
  • RequiresPricingCheck: If true, call GET /checkAvailability/{productPricesDetailsId}/pricingData with selected options before adding to cart
  • RequiresReservationCheck: If true, call GET /checkAvailability/{productPricesDetailsId}/reservation with selected options to confirm availability
  • Fields: Present options to users in the order returned. Required fields must be selected before proceeding
  • Addons: Secondary/Extra Fare fields represent optional add-ons that can increase the price

tags: Availability and Pricing - Cached Availability and Live pricing
get.summary: Get pricing data for selected options
get.description:
Confirm pricing data for a given fare/date/time. If RequiresPricingCheck=true in the results of GET /checkAvailability then this endpoint must be called before adding to cart. This endpoint returns the final pricing after all options (pickup, dropoff, addons) have been selected.

Response

Top Level Result Object

The response contains pricing information for the selected product fare with all options applied.

Field name Nullable Type Description
RRPnFloatRecommended Retail Price on this date+time in agent's currency. This is the price you should charge your customer.
NetRatenFloatAgent Net Rate on this date+time in agent's currency. This is your cost for the product.
FareCurrencyRRPnFloatRecommended Retail Price on this date+time in the fare's native currency (may differ from agent's currency).
FareCurrencyNetRatenFloatAgent Net Rate on this date+time in the fare's native currency (may differ from agent's currency).
addOnPricingnArray (Objects)Array of AddOnPricing objects containing pricing information for any selected add-ons or optional extras. Empty array if no add-ons are available or selected.

AddOnPricing Object

Contains pricing details for optional add-ons (e.g., photo packages, transfer costs, extra activities).

Field name Nullable Type Description
addonIdnStringIdentifier for the add-on option (matches externalId from availability check).
addonNamenStringDisplay name of the add-on.
RRPnFloatAdditional retail price for this add-on in agent's currency.
NetRatenFloatAdditional net cost for this add-on in agent's currency.
FareCurrencyRRPyFloatAdditional retail price for this add-on in fare's native currency.
FareCurrencyNetRateyFloatAdditional net cost for this add-on in fare's native currency.

Important Notes:

  • When to call: This endpoint must be called when RequiresPricingCheck=true is returned from GET /checkAvailability
  • Parameters: Use the bookingTimeId and bookingDate from the availability response, along with any selected options (pickup, dropoff, addons)
  • Currency: RRP and NetRate are in your agent's configured currency. FareCurrencyRRP and FareCurrencyNetRate are in the fare's original currency
  • Total pricing: The RRP and NetRate fields include the base fare price. Add-on pricing (if any) is additional and should be added to get the total price
  • Margin calculation: Your profit margin = RRP - NetRate
  • Add-ons: If customer selects add-ons (photo packages, transfers, etc.), include their pricing in your total calculation
  • Before checkout: Always call this endpoint to get the most up-to-date pricing before allowing customer to proceed to checkout

Typical Workflow:

  1. Call GET /checkAvailability to get available dates and options
  2. Customer selects date, time, pickup, dropoff, and any add-ons
  3. If RequiresPricingCheck=true, call this endpoint with selected options
  4. Display final pricing to customer (base RRP + addon RRP)
  5. If RequiresReservationCheck=true, call reservation endpoint before adding to cart
  6. Proceed to add to cart and checkout

get.description:
Get extra reservation data for a given set of parameters. If RequiresReservationCheck=true in the results of GET /checkAvailability then this endpoint must be called before adding to cart. This endpoint confirms final availability and retrieves any additional costs (such as transfer fees) that may apply based on selected pickup/dropoff locations.

Response

Top Level Result Object

The response contains reservation confirmation data and any additional costs that apply to the selected options.

Field name Nullable Type Description
transferyStringTransfer fee amount (if any) that applies to the selected pickup/dropoff combination. This is typically charged for specific pickup or dropoff locations. Value is in the fare's currency. Null if no transfer fee applies.
currencyyStringISO 4217 currency code for the fare (e.g., 'AUD', 'NZD', 'USD'). This is the currency that the transfer fee (if any) is denominated in.

Important Notes:

  • When to call: This endpoint must be called when RequiresReservationCheck=true is returned from GET /checkAvailability
  • Purpose: Confirms that the selected combination of date, time, pickup, and dropoff is valid and available
  • Transfer fees: Some pickup or dropoff locations may incur additional transfer charges. These are returned in the 'transfer' field
  • Currency handling: The transfer fee is in the fare's native currency. You may need to convert this to your agent's currency if they differ
  • Null values: If transfer is null, no additional transfer fee applies
  • Pricing calculation: Add any transfer fee to the base fare price (from pricingData endpoint) to get the total cost
  • Required parameters: pickupId and dropoffId are required if the product has pickup/dropoff options (check the Fields array from availability response)

Transfer Fee Examples:

  • Hotel pickup: Tours may charge extra for pickups from specific hotels or locations outside the main pickup zone
  • Airport transfers: Additional fees often apply for airport pickup/dropoff locations
  • Remote locations: Distant pickup points may incur surcharges
  • Premium locations: Some properties or venues may have special transfer pricing

Typical Workflow:

  1. Call GET /checkAvailability to get available dates and options
  2. Customer selects date, time, pickup, and dropoff locations
  3. If RequiresPricingCheck=true, call GET /checkAvailability/{productPricesDetailsId}/pricingData to get base pricing
  4. If RequiresReservationCheck=true, call this endpoint with all selected options
  5. Check response for transfer fees and add to total pricing
  6. Display final total to customer: Base Price + Addon Prices + Transfer Fee (if any)
  7. Proceed to add to cart and checkout

Error Handling:

  • If the endpoint returns an error, the selected combination may not be available
  • Customer should be prompted to select different pickup/dropoff options
  • Empty response {} indicates reservation check passed but no additional data needed

get.description:
Check availability of options in a package given a start date. Note, this assumes the start date is the selected available date for the first option in the package. Only subsequent options will be checked. For example, if the first option in the package has a duration of 'one' and start date is '01-Jan-2017', then the second option will be queried for start date + 1, that is, 02-Jan-2017. The first option will *not* be checked. #### Response #### **Top Level Result Object**
Field name Nullable Type Description
n/ayArray (Objects)Array of daily availability objects - keyed by Product Prices Details Id.
**Daily Availability Object**
Field name Nullable Type Description
BookingDatenStringDate availability for in format "01-Dec-2013".
EndDateyStringEnd date of the product/activity/tour (if available) in format "01-Dec-2013".
BookingTimeIDyStringID of the Booking time.
NumAvailablenIntegerPlaces available for that date. There are three types of return values:
  • -1: The tour does not run on this day.
  • 0: There is no availability remaining for this booking time on the given date.
  • >=1: There are X number of places available for this booking time on the given date.
DateRestrictionyObjectDate Restriction object.
***Date Restriction Object***
Field name Nullable Type Description
minimumConsecutiveDaysnIntegerDefault one. Minimum number of consecutive days (inclusive) that must be booked.
dateyStringDate restriction applies to.
canArrivenInteger"0" indicates not able to start on this date.
canDepartnInteger"0" indicates not able to finish on this date.

post.description:
Submit an order with products and customer details. This is typically the final step in the order placement workflow. ## Request Overview **Endpoint**: `POST /apiv1/order` **Authentication**: Bearer token required in Authorization header **Content-Type**: application/json ## Workflow Integration This endpoint is **Step 5** in the complete order placement workflow: 1. **Generate Booking Reference** → `POST /apiv1/bookingreference` 2. **Get Referral Sources** → `GET /apiv1/referralsources` (optional - depends on reseller) 3. **Get Payment Methods** → `GET /apiv1/agent/paymentmethods` 4. **Validate Cart** → `POST /apiv1/validateCart` 5. **Submit Order** → `POST /apiv1/order` ← **This Endpoint** #### Request Overview of the nested JSON request object. Refer to "parameters" section further below for detailed requirements at each level. **Order Request** ``` -> Product (one to many - Lists all Products used on the Order) -> Redeemer (one to many - Lists all Customers on the Order) -> Redeemer Product (one to many . Lists all Products per customer) -> Booking Details (one to many . Lists all Bookings per customer per Product) ``` **Product** Product(s) used in the Order. **Redeemer** Customer on this Order. Note that the first customer in the array is the primary customer. This will be the customer that is shown on search results. **Redeemer Product** Each customer's Product(s). **Booking Detail** Each customer's Product's booking information. ## Key Request Body Parameters:
Parameter Type Required? Description
bookingReference String true Booking reference for order. Obtain by calling POST /bookingreference.
agentReference String false Custom reference for order. May be used by external applications to identify orders.
paymentMethod String false Method of payment used. This value is a string, and should match one of the payment method options shown on the checkout page in TDMS.
emailVouchers Boolean false Send email with voucher to client. When used, please ensure that the first redeemer has a valid email address.
totalCharged Float true Gross amount of the entire order.
creditCardFee Float false Amount charged as a credit card fee.
processAsQuote Boolean false Whether to process this order as a Quote. If you want to save the order as a Quote, this must be done before requesting a payment link.
referralSourceId Integer false ID of order referral source (see GET /referralsources to get a list of referral sources). This field is compulsory for agents which have enabled this feature in TDMS.
products Array true Array of Product objects.
product.productPricesDetailsId Integer true ID of ProductPriceDetails (key to Product Detail).
product.qty Integer true Quantity of Product in Order.
product.packageId Integer false ID of associated Package. Should be used when adding a Package to an order.
product.apiProviderId Integer false ID of Api Provider. Should be used if product you are ordering came with this property in the initial request.
product.datePriceCacheId Integer false identifier of special price returned for booking date from availability lookup. Refer GET /checkavailabilityrange. Note: When booking multiple dates with different prices, a separate product will be required per different price.
product.priceOverRidden Boolean false The price override functionality is used when an agent negotiates a variation to a product with the supplier (for example, on a multi-day tour the customer might finish 2 days earlier than the rest of the group, and the supplier would agree on a reduced rate for this with the agent).
product.agentRRP Float false Adjusted RRP of product. Should only be used when there is a price override in effect.
product.adjustedAgentNR Float false Adjusted net rate of product. Should only be used when there is a price override in effect.
product.discountClaimReason String false Reason for price override. Should only be used when there is a price override in effect.
product.staffContactName String false Supplier staff contact name for price override. Should only be used when there is a price override in effect.
payments Array false Array of Payment objects.
payment.amount float true Amount of payment.
payment.journalAccountId integer false ID of payment type (journalAccountId from /agent/paymentmethods route). Either journalAccountId or paymentMethod must be set.
payment.paymentMethod String false ID of payment type (code from /agent/paymentmethods route). Either journalAccountId or paymentMethod must be set.
payment.reference String false Reference number of online credit card payment.
redeemers Array true Array of Redeemer objects.
redeemer.emailAddress String false Email address.
redeemer.firstName String true First name.
redeemer.lastName String true Last (surname) name.
redeemer.phone String false Phone number.
redeemer.redeemerCountry Integer true ISO 3166-1 numeric country code.
redeemer.dateOfBirth Date false Date of birth of the redeemers, format : d-M-Y (ex : 1-Jan-1990)
redeemer.title String false Title. Must be chosen from ['Master', 'Mr', 'Mrs', 'Ms', 'Miss', 'Mx']
redeemer.products Array true Array of Redeemer Product Objects.
redeemer.product.productPricesDetailsId Integer true ID of ProductPriceDetails (primary key to Product Detail).
redeemer.product.redeemerQty Integer true Quantity of Product for this Redeemer.
redeemer.product.bookWithFirstRedeemer Boolean false If true, subsequent customer details will be booked with the primary customer on eligible products/fares.
redeemer.product.bookings Array true Array of Booking Detail Objects.
redeemer.product.booking.bookingComment String false Comment on this Booking.
redeemer.product.booking.travelDate String false Travel date of this Booking in format '01-Dec-2013'.
redeemer.product.booking.datePriceCacheId Integer false identifier of special price returned for booking date from availability lookup. Refer GET /checkavailabilityrange
redeemer.product.booking.timeId Integer false Time ID of Booking.
redeemer.product.booking.pickupId Integer false ID of pickup details.
redeemer.product.booking.commences String false Commences.
redeemer.product.booking.pickupTime String false Selected time of pickup.
redeemer.product.booking.pickupLocation String false Selected location of pickup.
redeemer.product.booking.dropoffId Integer false ID of dropoff details.
redeemer.product.booking.dropoffLocation String false Selected location of dropoff.
redeemer.product.booking.dropoffTime String false Selected time of dropoff.
redeemer.product.booking.bookingDetailsComment String false Booking manifest/voucher comments.
redeemer.product.optionalFields Array false Array of Optional Fields.
redeemer.product.optionalFields.optionalFieldId Integer true Unique Id of the optional field.
redeemer.product.optionalFields.fieldText String true Text customer has entered for the optional field
#### Request Overview of the nested JSON request object. Refer to "parameters" section further below for detailed requirements at each level. **Order Request** ``` -> Product (one to many - Lists all Products used on the Order) -> Redeemer (one to many - Lists all Customers on the Order) -> Redeemer Product (one to many . Lists all Products per customer) -> Booking Details (one to many . Lists all Bookings per customer per Product) ``` **Product** Product(s) used in the Order. **Redeemer** Customer on this Order. Note that the first customer in the array is the primary customer. This will be the customer that is shown on search results. **Redeemer Product** Each customer's Product(s). **Booking Detail** Each customer's Product's booking information. #### Response **Top Level Result Object** | Field name | Nullable | Type | Description | |------------|----------|------|-------------| | id | n | Integer | ID of the created Order. | | messages | n | Array (string) | Non-critical advisory messages.
Order has been accepted but some aspects were incomplete. eg Unable to send emails, Issues with eBookings encountered.
An empty array indicates everything was successful | | eBookings | n | Object | eBookings object. | | vouchers | n | Object | Vouchers object. | **eBookings Object** | Field name | Nullable | Type | Description | |------------|----------|------|-------------| | bookingError | n | Boolean | Error indicator.
**false** - All clear

get.summary: Get cancellation fees for an order
get.description:
## Get cancellation fees for an order ##

Get a list of all products included on the order, with cancellation fees and other information required to cancel the order.

#### Response #### **Top Level Result** Array of OrdersProduct objects. Each object represents one of the ProductPricesDetails that is included in the order. **OrdersProducts Object**
Field name Nullable Type Description
ordersProductIdnIntegerID of the OrdersProduct.
productPricesDetailIdnIntegerID of the ProductPricesDetail this OrdersProduct is for.
voucherNumbernStringVoucher number of this OrdersProduct.
isRefundablenBooleanWhether this OrdersProduct can be refunded.
totalQtynIntegerTotal quantity of this ProductPricesDetail included in the order.
cancellationFeesnArray (Objects)Array of CancellationFee objects.
redeemersnArray (Objects)Array of Redeemer objects.
**CancellationFee Object**

A CancellationFee consists of an amount and a cutoff date/time when cancellation must be made for that amount to be in effect. For example, if you have three cancellation fees: [{"amountInSupplierCurrency":17000,"cutoffDate":null},
{"amountInSupplierCurrency":1200,"cutoffDate":"2014-01-01T00:00:00+1200"},
{"amountInSupplierCurrency":0,"cutoffDate":"2013-12-25T00:00:00+1200"}]
This OrdersProduct can be cancelled without any supplier fees prior to 25 December, with a $1200 cancellation fee until 1 January, and with a $17000 cancellation fee after that time.

If a travel date was supplied when the order was made, each CancellationFee will have a cutoffDate which indicates the time at which it will no longer be available. If no travel date was supplied, the CancellationFees will instead have a daysBeforeTravelDate field which the client can use to calculate cancellation fees once the travel date is known.

Cancellation fees are calculated in the supplier's currency. For convenience, they are also converted into your currency using the current exchange rate, but as WT updates our exchange rates daily please be aware that these amounts will not be accurate if the cancellation is not processed immediately.

Field name Nullable Type Description
amountInSupplierCurrencynFloatAmount of supplier cancellation fee in supplier's currency.
supplierCurrencyyStringSupplier's currency code (ISO-4217 three-character code).
amountInAgentCurrencynFloatAmount of supplier cancellation fee in agent's currency. Note that exchange rates used by the WT system are updated daily. The values shown will be in effect for cancellations which are processed immediately, but should not be cached or used for delayed cancellations.
cutoffDateyStringDate/time cutoff when this cancellation fee will no longer be available, in ISO 8601 format e.g. '2013-12-12T23:00:46+0000'.
daysBeforeTravelDateyIntegerNumber of days before travel date when this cancellation fee will no longer be available.
**Redeemer Object**

A redeemer is a customer who was booked on the OrdersProduct. Each OrdersProduct/redeemer combination has a unique redeemerId - if the redeemer has booked multiple products on the same order, they will have a different redeemerId for each of these products. The redeemerId must be provided when performing a partial cancellation on the order.

Field name Nullable Type Description
redeemerIdnIntegerID of Redeemer.
namenStringFull name of redeemer (first name + last name).
qtynIntegerQuantity of ProductPricesDetail booked for this redeemer.
bookingsnArray (Objects)Array of RedeemerBookings for this redeemer.
**RedeemerBooking Object**

A RedeemerBooking is a booking made for a Redeemer. A Redeemer will have one RedeemerBooking for each travel date that they have booked.

Field name Nullable Type Description
bookingIdnIntegerUnique ID of this RedeemerBooking.
travelDatenStringIntended travel date in ISO 8601 format e.g. '2013-12-12 T23:00:46+0000'.

get.summary: Get available accommodation products by location
get.description:
## Get available accommodation products by location ##

Retrieve a list of available accommodation products filtered by location parameters. This endpoint allows you to search for accommodation based on country, state, zone, property type, and other filters. It supports both room and dorm searches with customizable occupancy parameters.

The search requires mandatory location filters (country and state) and date range parameters. Additional filters such as zone, property type, and search keywords can be applied to narrow down results. The response includes pagination support and sorting options. Use combinations of routes /accommodation/countries /accommodation/states /accommodation/zones to determine Ids to pass to this route OR given a countryId from /accommodation/countries use /accommodation/locations with a search term to find all States/Zones matching the search term

#### Response #### **Top Level Result Object**
Field name Nullable Type Description
accommodationResultsnArray (Objects)Array of accommodation product objects.
apiProviderAvailabilityyArray (Objects)Array of API provider availability results.
totalRecordsnIntegerTotal number of accommodation products found.
fetchedRecordsnIntegerNumber of records returned in this response.
**Accommodation Product Object**
Field name Nullable Type Description
productIdnIntegerUnique identifier for the accommodation product.
namenStringName of the accommodation property.
productImagePathyStringURL to the main product image.
productTeaseryStringShort description of the accommodation.
addressyObjectAddress information for the property.
categoriesnObjectLocation and accommodation type categories.
farespricesnArray (Objects)Array of available fare price options.
availabilityyObjectReal-time availability information if applicable.

tags.name: Availability and Pricing - Booking system based Live Availability
tags.description:
Real-time availability checking, pricing data, reservation management, and supplier communication for products.

--

## Booking System Workflows

Different booking systems require different API call sequences based on their capabilities and requirements. Follow the appropriate workflow based on the `farePrices.bookingSystem` value:

### 🔄 Workflow A: Standard Booking Systems
**For `farePrices.bookingSystem` = `Generic`, `Clockwork`, `CMS`, or `SOAAccommodation`**

```
1. GET /apiv1/bookingdetails/{productPricesDetailsId}
              ↓
2. GET /apiv1/checkavailabilityrange/{productPricesDetailsId}/{timeId}/{startDate}/{days}?cached=1
```

**Step-by-step:**
1. **Get Booking Details** - Call `/bookingdetails` to retrieve available booking times, pickup locations, and any optional fields
2. **Check Availability** - Use `/checkavailabilityrange` with the timeId from step 1 to get real-time availability and pricing
   - Add `?cached=1` parameter to get cached response for faster performance

### 🔄 Workflow B: Enhanced Booking Systems  
**For `farePrices.bookingSystem` = `Respaxv2`, `IBIS`, or `CustomLinc`**

```
1. GET /apiv1/checkavailabilityrange/{productPricesDetailsId}/0/{startDate}/{days}?cached=1
              ↓
2. GET /apiv1/bookingdetails/detailsToConfirmAvailability/{productPricesDetailsId}/{date}/{timeId}
```

**Step-by-step:**
1. **Check Availability First** - Call `/checkavailabilityrange` with `timeId=0` to get initial availability and pricing
   - Add `?cached=1` parameter to get cached response for faster performance
2. **Get Confirmation Details** - Use `/detailsToConfirmAvailability` to retrieve additional details required for booking confirmation (pickup/dropoff locations, special requirements)

### 📋 Booking System Reference

| Booking System | Workflow | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Generic` | **A** | Standard generic booking system |
| `Clockwork` | **A** | Clockwork reservation system |  
| `CMS` | **A** | Content Management System bookings |
| `SOAAccommodation` | **A** | Service-oriented accommodation system |
| `Respaxv2` | **B** | Respax version 2 booking system |
| `IBIS` | **B** | IBIS reservation system |
| `CustomLinc` | **B** | Custom Linc booking platform |

### 🎯 Implementation Tips

- **Always check `farePrices.bookingSystem`** before choosing your workflow
- **Handle timeId properly**: Some systems provide multiple time options
- **Use cached responses**: Add `?cached=1` parameter to `/checkavailabilityrange` for faster performance
  - `cached=1` returns cached availability data when available
  - Improves response time and reduces server load
  - Recommended for most use cases unless real-time data is critical
- **Error handling**: Both workflows support proper error responses
- **Caching**: Results can be cached based on your implementation needs
- **Required fields**: Pay attention to `RequiresFurtherDetailsToConfirm` flags in responses

Order Placement
tags.description:
Place quote or order for products in the cart, supporting both offline and online payment workflows. This section includes all endpoints needed for the complete order creation process, from generating booking references to validating carts and processing payments.

--

## Complete Order Creation Workflow

This section contains all endpoints needed for the complete order placement process, supporting both offline and online payment workflows.
                   
### 📋 Workflow Overview

**Core Steps (Both Payment Types):**
1. **POST /apiv1/bookingreference** - Generate unique booking reference
2. **GET /apiv1/referralsources** - Get available referral sources *(optional - depends on reseller)*
3. **GET /apiv1/agent/paymentmethods** - Get available payment methods
4. **POST /apiv1/validateCart** - Validate cart contents before submission
5. **POST /apiv1/order** - Submit the quote or order (use `processAsQuote` flag to determine workflow)

**Additional Steps for Online Payments:**
6. **GET /apiv1/validateQuote** - Validate quote after submission
7. **GET /apiv1/pgw/authenticate** - Get payment gateway URL  
8. **POST /apiv1/order/{bookingReference}/convertQuote** - Convert paid quote to order

---

### 💳 Payment Workflow Types

#### Offline Payment Flow
```
Booking Reference → Referrals (Optional) → Payment Methods → Validate Cart → Submit Order → Complete
          ↓              ↓                       ↓                  ↓              ↓
       (step 1)      (step 2)                (step 3)            (step 4)        (step 5)
```

- Direct order creation with `processAsQuote: false`
- Payment handled externally (cash, bank transfer, etc.)
- Immediate order confirmation
- Uses steps 1-5 only

#### Online Payment Flow
```
Booking Reference → Referrals (Optional) → Payment Methods → Validate Cart → Submit Quote → Validate Quote → Payment Gateway → Convert to Order
          ↓              ↓                      ↓              ↓                ↓               ↓                 ↓                 ↓
       (step 1)      (step 2)               (step 3)        (step 4)         (step 5)        (step 6)           (step 7)         (step 8)
```

- Quote creation with `processAsQuote: true`
- Payment gateway integration
- Quote-to-order conversion after payment
- Uses all steps 1-8

---

### ⚡ Quick Start Examples

**Offline Payment:**
1. Get booking reference
2. Get referral sources & payment methods
3. Validate cart contents
4. Submit order with offline payment method

**Online Payment:**  
1-4. Same as offline
5. Submit as quote
6. Validate quote
7. Get payment URL & redirect customer
8. Convert quote to order after successful payment

---

### 🔄 Error Handling

All endpoints include proper error responses and validation:
- **Cart Validation**: Prevents invalid orders
- **Quote Validation**: Ensures order integrity  
- **Payment Validation**: Handles payment failures
- **Retry Mechanisms**: Support for error recovery

---

### 📊 Visual Workflows

**For interactive flowcharts and detailed visual workflows:**

🔗 **[View Order Placement Flowcharts →](/api/workflows/order-placement)**

The visual workflow page includes:
- Interactive Mermaid flowcharts for both payment types
- Step-by-step breakdowns with API endpoint details  
- Error handling visualization
- Side-by-side workflow comparisons
- Quick start implementation guides

Post-order management and customer service operations for completed orders.

--

This section provides endpoints for managing orders after they have been placed and confirmed:

**📋 Order Information & Vouchers:**
- Retrieve voucher details and order information
- Access customer order details for support purposes
- Download vouchers in multiple formats (JSON, HTML, PDF)

**💰 Cancellation & Refunds:**
- Get supplier cancellation fees before processing cancellations
- Cancel orders with proper fee calculations
- Handle refunds and order modifications

**🎯 Typical Post-Order Workflow:**
1. **GET /apiv1/voucher/{orderId}.json** - Get voucher/order details
2. **GET /apiv1/customerOrderDetail** - Search and retrieve customer order information
3. **GET /apiv1/order/{bookingReference}/supplierCancellationFees** - Check cancellation fees
4. **POST /apiv1/order/{bookingReference}/cancel** - Cancel order if needed

This section provides all accommodation-related functionality including:

**🏨 Location Management:**
- Get accommodation countries, states, and zones
- Search accommodation locations by criteria
- Retrieve accommodation property types

**🔍 Accommodation Search:**
- Search accommodation products by location
- Filter by property types and availability
- Get detailed accommodation information

**🎯 Typical Accommodation Workflow:**
1. **GET /apiv1/accommodation/countries** - Get available countries
2. **GET /apiv1/accommodation/states/{countryId}** - Get states for country
3. **GET /apiv1/accommodation/zones/{stateId}** - Get zones for state (optional)
4. **GET /apiv1/accommodation/types** - Get accommodation property types
5. **GET /apiv1/accommodation/locations** - Search accommodation locations
6. **GET /apiv1/accommodation/search** - Search accommodation products
